title: AI Adoption Index — U.S. vs China
subtitle: Public proxies for AI adoption inside the U.S. and Chinese economies — not a complete measure of total usage.
description: A two-proxy composite index approximating economy-wide AI adoption. Combines enterprise AI adoption rates (McKinsey State of AI 2025 + Stanford AI Index 2025) with industrial automation density (IFR World Robotics 2024, 2023 data). Neither proxy alone is a perfect measure of AI usage; together they provide a transparent, country-comparable directional signal.

enterprise_adoption.note: McKinsey State of AI 2025 (May 2025), North America respondents; corroborated by Stanford HAI AI Index 2025 (April 2025)

robot_density.note: IFR World Robotics 2024 (2023 data), United States

enterprise_adoption.note: Estimated from McKinsey State of AI 2025 global/regional data and CAICT AI White Paper 2024. McKinsey does not break out China separately; Stanford AI Index 2025 shows broadly comparable rates. Treat as directional.

robot_density.note: IFR World Robotics 2024 (2023 data), China — overtook Japan and Germany in robot density ranking; up from 392 in 2022

interpretive_sentence: Chinese firms and institutions show stronger visible AI adoption on these proxies — driven primarily by significantly higher industrial automation density in manufacturing. Enterprise AI adoption rates are broadly comparable between the two countries (composite index gap: 12.0 points, China ahead).

composite_construction.method: Weighted average of normalized proxy scores. Enterprise adoption is already 0-100 (% of organizations). Robot density is normalized as (value / 600) x 100 where 600 robots/10K workers is the normalization reference (above leading non-outlier economies: China 470, Germany 429, Japan 419; South Korea ~1,012 excluded as outlier). Default weights: enterprise 55%, robot density 45%.

enterprise_adoption.source_name: McKinsey & Company, The State of AI 2025
enterprise_adoption.supplementary: Stanford HAI AI Index 2025 (April 2025); CAICT White Paper on China's AI Development 2024 (China figure)
enterprise_adoption.definition: % of organizations using AI in at least one business function
enterprise_adoption.update_cadence: Annual (McKinsey: typically May; Stanford AI Index: typically April)

robot_density.source_name: International Federation of Robotics (IFR), World Robotics 2024
robot_density.definition: Installed industrial robots per 10,000 manufacturing workers
robot_density.update_cadence: Annual (typically published in October; next: October 2025 for 2024 data)

methodology_note: This index uses a multi-proxy approach because no single public data source provides a clean, symmetric, and automatable measure of AI adoption in both the U.S. and Chinese economies. The enterprise survey proxy (McKinsey 2025 + Stanford AI Index 2025) covers large firms and is comparable in intent but has limited China-specific granularity. The robot density proxy (IFR 2024) is highly symmetric and verifiable but measures industrial automation broadly, not AI specifically. The composite score is a transparent, directional proxy — not a definitive measure of national AI adoption.
coverage_note: Enterprise adoption (China): estimated from McKinsey 2025 regional data, CAICT surveys, and Stanford AI Index 2025 — confidence is medium; treat as directional. Robot density: high confidence for both countries — IFR uses the same methodology for all countries. Composite is valid for directional U.S.-vs-China comparison.
what_this_does_not_capture: Consumer AI usage (individuals using AI tools, apps, or devices)
AI usage by small and medium enterprises
AI application quality or depth of integration
Software-only AI deployments not captured in industrial robot density
Sector-specific AI adoption in financial services, healthcare, or services
AI adoption among Chinese firms that do not file with the SEC

sources.name: McKinsey & Company, The State of AI 2025
International Federation of Robotics (IFR), World Robotics 2024
